name: GT-sgn-prop-P
source: Kitaev arXiv:2405.02434, refs/kitaev-2405.02434/approximate_algebras.tex:517-535 (sgn and Proposition prop_P, the spectral-idempotent construction). VERBATIM: "|X|=(X^2)^{1/2},\qquad \sgn(X)=X(X^2)^{-1/2}\qquad\quad" ... "Let $P$ be an element of a Banach algebra such that $\|P^2-P\|\le\delta<1/4$, and let" ... "\wt{P}=\theta(2P-I),\qquad" ... "\text{where}\quad \theta(X)=\frac{1}{2}\bigl(I+\sgn(X)\bigr)." ... "Then $\wt{P}^2=\wt{P}$,\, $\wt{P}$ commutes with $P$, and $\|\wt{P}-P\|\le \|2P-I\|\ts O(\delta)$." This fixes the CONSTRUCTION used here (def-spectral-idempotent): sgn(S)=S (S^2)^{-1/2} = S R, and theta(2X-I)=(1/2)(I+sgn(S)) with S=2X-I. It is purely a NAME for these algebra expressions; we use it ONLY to expand theta and sgn in nodes N1.5/N1.6, NOT for its idempotence content. Applied for X=F (in algebra A) and X=Phi (in algebra B).
